store.name: World Vibe Web
store.tagline: The distributed app store for vibe-coded projects.

featured.headline: FAN FAVORITE
featured.title: The prompt library the internet built.
featured.subtitle: With 150K+ GitHub stars, Prompts.chat is where developers, writers, and AI power users come to find the perfect words — open source, self-hostable, and free forever.

featured.headline: JUST SHIPPED
featured.title: Speak freely. Let your Mac follow.
featured.subtitle: Textream tracks every word you say in real time, scrolling your script as you speak — all processed on-device, tucked inside a stunning Dynamic Island overlay.

featured.headline: RISING STAR
featured.title: Your Mac's vitals, always at a glance.
featured.subtitle: DodoPulse tucks gorgeous real-time graphs for CPU, memory, GPU, and network right into your menu bar — lightweight, native Swift, and surprisingly beautiful.

featured.headline: TRENDING
featured.title: X-ray vision for your Hono API.
featured.subtitle: Drop one middleware into your Hono app and instantly get a real-time dashboard for requests, exceptions, logs, and database queries — zero config, full clarity.

apps.name: Agent Chess
apps.subtitle: Local chess matches for AI agents
apps.description: Run a local chess game where two AI agents join, play moves, and follow a live board.
apps.longDescription: Agent Chess is a TypeScript CLI for pitting two AI agents against each other in a local chess game. Agents join the same match, submit moves one turn at a time, can negotiate draws, and can watch the live board in a local web UI.
features: Two AI agents can join the same local game and play turn by turn
Built-in draw request and accept flow
Live local web UI for board state, move history, and replay

apps.name: Agentlytics
apps.subtitle: AI Coding Agent Analytics
apps.description: Comprehensive analytics dashboard for AI coding agents — Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, VS Code Copilot, Zed, Antigravity, OpenCode, Command Code. One command to turn scattered AI conversations into a unified analytics dashboard.
apps.longDescription: You switch between Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, VS Code Copilot, and more — each with its own siloed conversation history. Agentlytics gives you a full picture with one command: npx agentlytics. Dashboard with KPIs, activity heatmap, editor breakdown, coding streaks, token economy. Session search and full conversation replay. Cost estimation broken down by model, editor, project. Team context sharing via Relay with MCP integration. 100% local — no data ever leaves your machine.
features: Dashboard with KPIs and activity heatmaps
Cost estimation by model and editor
100% local — no data leaves your machine

apps.name: AI Word or Not

apps.description: Can you tell the difference between AI-generated and human-written text? Test your skills with hundreds of challenges across multiple difficulty levels.

apps.name: AI2image
apps.subtitle: AI image generation platform
apps.description: Generate stunning images with AI. Create Studio Ghibli style, realistic, and artistic images using AI prompts.

apps.name: AI2SQL
apps.subtitle: Generate SQL queries with AI
apps.description: AI-powered SQL query generator. Write complex SQL without knowing SQL syntax. Supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and more.
apps.longDescription: AI2SQL helps developers, data analysts, and non-technical users generate SQL queries using natural language. Paste your schema, describe what you want, get production-ready SQL. Supports multiple databases including MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MongoDB, and Oracle.

apps.name: Appétit
apps.subtitle: Bon Appétit for Apps
apps.description: An Apple App Store-inspired catalog of free, tiny apps built with agentic engineering and vibecoding. Pure HTML/CSS/JS — no frameworks, no build step.
apps.longDescription: Appétit is an open-source, JSON-driven app store clone that showcases your projects in a beautiful, browsable catalog. Features a dark/light theme, sidebar navigation, featured carousel, app detail pages with screenshots, brew/npx install modals, instant search, and GitHub star counts. All content lives in a single apps.json file — edit it and deploy. Built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JS using agentic engineering and vibecoding. No frameworks, no build step, deploys anywhere as static files.
features: Dark and light theme with system preference
Featured carousel, app cards, detail pages
Brew / npx install modals with clipboard copy
Built with agentic engineering and vibecoding

_comments.title: Awesome
_comments.body: Awesome app!

apps.name: asc
apps.subtitle: App Store Connect from your terminal & AI agents
apps.description: A Swift CLI for managing iOS and macOS apps on App Store Connect — builds, releases, TestFlight, subscriptions, screenshots, and more. Full AI-agent support via CAEOAS affordances.
apps.longDescription: asc is a command-line tool that brings the full power of App Store Connect to your terminal. Automate builds, releases, TestFlight distribution, subscriptions, in-app purchases, screenshots, app previews, code signing, and customer reviews — all from a single CLI. Every command outputs structured JSON with CAEOAS affordances, so AI agents can navigate the entire release workflow without knowing the command tree. Works in CI pipelines (GitHub Actions) and supports multi-account authentication.
features: List apps, create versions, link builds, submit for App Store review
Archive Xcode projects, export IPA/PKG, upload to App Store Connect
Manage TestFlight beta groups, testers, and beta review submissions
In-app purchases, subscriptions, offers, and offer codes
AI-powered App Store screenshot generation via Gemini
Code signing: bundle IDs, certificates, devices, profiles
Executable plugin system for custom event handlers
CAEOAS affordances — AI agents navigate without knowing the command tree

apps.name: check-ai
apps.subtitle: AI-Readiness Audit for Repos
apps.description: Audit any repository for AI-readiness. One command. 66 checks. Zero dependencies. Scans for agent configs, repo hygiene, grounding docs, testing safety nets, prompt templates, MCP integrations, AI dependencies — and scores it all on a 0–10 scale.
apps.longDescription: check-ai runs 66 checks grouped into 8 sections: Repo Hygiene, Grounding Docs, Testing Safety Net, Agent Configs, AI Context, Prompts & Skills, MCP integrations, and AI Dependencies. Each check is weighted by real-world impact and normalized to a 0–10 score with letter grades. Detects configs for Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini, Aider, and more. Interactive terminal output with animated score bars, or machine-readable JSON for CI pipelines. Zero dependencies — built entirely with Node.js built-ins.

Choose any system font for clock numerals, customize background and clock colors, and enjoy a smooth analog clock with hour, minute, and second hands. Supports English and Turkish localization. Universal binary for Apple Silicon and Intel.
features: Smooth analog clock with hour, minute, and second hands
All system fonts available for clock numerals

apps.name: Deeper
apps.subtitle: Messaging Analytics for Beeper
apps.description: A macOS messaging analytics app for Beeper — visualize your conversations across every platform, with on-device AI. See who you talk to most, discover your active hours, find ghosting patterns, explore group dynamics.
apps.longDescription: Deeper connects to your local Beeper Desktop app and turns your messaging data into beautiful, interactive analytics. It merges contacts across platforms (iMessage, WhatsApp, Instagram, Telegram, Signal, X/Twitter, and more), analyzes sent vs received message patterns, and visualizes everything with native macOS charts and Liquid Glass effects. Uses Apple Intelligence (on-device) for conversation summaries — no cloud, no API keys. All data stays on your machine.
apps.requirements: macOS 26 Tahoe or later, Beeper Desktop
features: Word frequency analysis and word clouds
Liquid Glass UI with native macOS 26 design

apps.name: Env Protector
apps.subtitle: Keep .env secrets hidden before they reach the editor.
apps.description: A VS Code extension that intercepts environment files and lets you view or manage them safely.
apps.longDescription: Env Protector is a VS Code extension built around a security-first workflow for `.env` files. Instead of masking secrets after a file opens, it intercepts access before rendering, offers a masked view, and lets you add, edit, or remove variables without exposing raw values during screen sharing or recordings.
features: Intercept .env file opening before secrets render on screen
Masked virtual view for reviewing keys without exposing values
Add, edit, and remove variables without opening the raw file
Workspace-level hiding of sensitive env files in the explorer

_comments.title: README Dosyası Ekleyelim

Repo'yu ziyaret eden biri oyunun ne olduğunu, nasıl oynanacağını veya nasıl çalıştırılacağını bilemiyor. GitHub'da hiç açıklama yok.

Çözüm:
Repo'nun ana klasörüne bir `README.md` dosyası ekleyelim:

```markdown
# Galaksinin Muhafızları 🚀

Galaksiyi düşmanlardan koru!

Nasıl Oynanır?
- WASD

apps.name: git-rewrite-commits

apps.description: AI-powered git commit message rewriter using Ollama or GPT. Automatically rewrite your entire git commit history with better, conventional commit messages.

Always creates backups before rewriting.

apps.name: Avalon
apps.subtitle: Visual prompt IDE for AI editing, reverse engineering, and image workflows
apps.description: Edit structured prompts, import community examples, reverse engineer images, and generate visuals from one privacy-first workspace.
apps.longDescription: Avalon is a privacy-first visual prompt IDE for image generation and other AI workflows. Build and manage prompts as structured JSON in a tree editor, ask an AI assistant to rewrite only the field you are focused on, expand rough ideas into production-ready prompt structures, reverse engineer uploaded images into reusable prompts, browse prompts.chat and import community examples, then generate images with fal.ai or Wiro.ai. Prompts persist locally, API keys stay in sessionStorage, and the app can be installed as a PWA for a more native feel.
apps.requirements: Modern browser; your own OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google API key for AI features. Optional fal.ai or Wiro.ai API key for image generation.
features: Visual tree editor for nested JSON prompt editing
Natural-language AI assistant that rewrites only the selected field
Prompt expander that returns scene, style, subjects, palette, composition, and negative guidance
Reverse engineer uploaded images into reusable prompts with confidence and model hints
Browse prompts.chat and import community prompts into your local prompt library
Generate images with fal.ai or Wiro.ai using model and size or aspect controls
Local-first prompt storage with session-only API key handling
Installable PWA with responsive dashboard and editor layouts

It supports interactive first-run setup, dynamic input prompts generated from model schemas, multiple auth header modes, WebSocket progress with polling fallback, automatic output downloads with readable filenames, and both npm and direct binary installation flows. Secrets are stored in macOS Keychain when available, with a local fallback store for other environments.

apps.name: superportless
apps.subtitle: Keep the exact same localhost origin while switching worktrees per tab.
apps.description: A local dev router that lets each browser tab connect to a different server without changing host or port.
apps.longDescription: vercel/portless is a clever idea, but subdomains change the origin and often force updates to CORS settings, OAuth redirect URLs, and environment configs. superportless takes a different approach: each browser tab can connect to a different local server while keeping the exact same host and port, such as localhost:3000. That means no subdomains, no origin change, and no application config changes. Just open multiple tabs and test multiple worktrees in parallel.
apps.requirements: macOS or Linux. Source builds require Go 1.21+ and a C compiler.
features: Each browser tab can route to a different local server while keeping the same localhost host and port
Avoids subdomains and origin changes that would otherwise affect CORS, OAuth redirects, and environment settings
Lets you test multiple worktrees in parallel without teaching the application about the routing tool
Includes Next.js-specific support for HMR and /api/* routing during development

apps.name: Trip Invoice

apps.name: VitePress Mermaid Renderer
apps.subtitle: Interactive Mermaid diagrams for VitePress docs.
apps.description: A VitePress plugin that turns Mermaid code blocks into interactive diagrams with export and theme support.
apps.longDescription: Transform static Mermaid diagrams into interactive visualizations inside VitePress. The plugin adds zooming, panning, fullscreen mode, code copy, theme-aware rendering, and export options for documentation sites that need more than a static SVG.
apps.requirements: A VitePress project with Mermaid diagrams
features: Zoom, pan, and reset controls for complex Mermaid diagrams
Fullscreen mode for distraction-free diagram review
Theme-aware rendering that follows light and dark mode
Export diagrams as SVG, PNG, or JPG

_comments.title: const mermaidRenderer <-- why?
_comments.body: As best I can tell the const variable assignment is unused. I can't see any mention of a reason for the assignment or if I'm supposed to do something with it. Perhaps you could help alleviate this confusion for me?
